text.text:
I found out about Mamie through TikTok, and it was absolutely worth the hype. I’m so glad we decided to check it out.

Yes, it’s on the pricier side, but completely worth it. The ingredients tasted incredibly fresh and were packed full of flavor; especially the meats. You can really tell they don’t cut corners on quality. The focaccia bread was fantastic and truly tied the whole sandwich together.

The inside is pretty small with limited seating, but there is outdoor seating available right on the sidewalk. I appreciated that the outdoor area was fenced in. It made it feel a little more private and comfortable despite being near the street.

My husband and I split the Verona sandwich and the Pane & Pistachio dessert sandwich. It was more than enough for lunch. The portions are generous. My husband absolutely loved the dessert. It was a fun and unique addition to the meal.

It’s also conveniently located right by a bus stop, which made it super easy to hop on the bus after we finished eating.

Overall, I would definitely go here again. Totally worth the visit!

text.text:
Paid meter street parking. Sucks but it’s worth it.

We have to drive out 1-2 hours to get here. We usually go if we’re doing something in the area.

We tried Di Parma first and fell in love. We’ve been hesitant to try any other sandwich here after that. We tried Riccione recently and it was good but not as good as Di Parma. It’s an expensive sandwich but you get what you pay for. A sandwich can be split between 2 people.

Great bread and creamy sandwiches. Pistachios in the di Parma add texture and a mild nutty flavor.

It’s a small shop which I can see getting busy at peak hours. I’ve always gone near closing and parking is easy to find. I usually order for pick up for efficiency.

text.text:
The Best Focaccia Sandwiches in LA – A Bread Lover’s Dream!
This was my first time at Mamie, though my wife and son are already regulars here, and now I completely understand why.
The Secret is in the Bread:
The star of the show is undoubtedly their 72-hour fermented focaccia-style bread. It is so incredibly light, airy, and flavorful that I jokingly told my family, "You could put me inside this bread and even I’d taste delicious!" It is truly the foundation of a perfect sandwich.
* Viali - Di Parma & Viali - Di Guanciale: My wife and son love the sweet-and-savory combination, so they went with these options featuring jam/preserves. They were both thrilled with their choices.
* Rimini: I opted for the Rimini, and it was outstanding.
The sandwiches are quite large and very filling—definitely great value for the quality you're getting.
One thing I loved was finding the classic glass-bottle Coca-Cola here. Having worked with a Coca-Cola production plant in Turkey in the past, a production manager once explained to me why these glass bottles (the ones requiring an opener) taste better: it’s all about the pressure. These bottles hold a higher carbonation pressure compared to plastic or cans, which truly preserves the crispness and flavor. It makes a world of difference!
Whether you're a first-timer like me or a regular, Mamie never misses. Authentic flavors, high-quality ingredients, and that unforgettable bread. Highly recommended!

text.text: I get the hype. They truly do things special here. The house-made bread already puts them ahead of everyone else. And they combine flavors and textures well—a creamy cheese spread with bright fruit jam with acidic arugula touch and meaty prosciutto. How can you go wrong? And I think the portion size is just right for me, not too little and not too much where I take some leftover home (but maybe a part of me wants that). Seating was decent with about 5 tables inside (for pairs). Parking wasn’t too bad either in the adjacent neighborhood.

text.text:
Mamie Sandwiches in West Hollywood has easily become one of our favorite sandwich spots in Los Angeles — especially when we’re in the area. It’s honestly way better than most of the overhyped chains around town. The bread alone is worth the visit: super crunchy on the outside, fresh, and perfect for holding everything together without getting soggy.

The portion sizes are phenomenal for the price, which feels rare these days. My go-to order is the Eros (Salame Toscano, crema di parmigiana, artichoke cream, spicy roasted eggplant, baby arugula) — it’s packed with flavor and just hits every time.

We also tried the Diva (mortadella, stracciatella, chopped pistachio, balsamic glaze, baby arugula). It was good, but not quite my favorite. The stracciatella was a bit overpowering and the sandwich needed more of a kick. We asked for a side of pesto and added it ourselves, which made a huge difference and brought everything together nicely.

The space itself is cute and thoughtfully designed, but it’s on the corner of a busy street. The patio is tiny and narrow, and there isn’t a ton of seating inside, so it’s better suited for grab-and-go than lingering. Today there was no line, but usually there is a line out the door.

Overall though, Mamie is absolutely worth the stop — great quality, generous portions, and genuinely delicious sandwiches. We’ll definitely keep coming back.
